settings.help: Save the game automatically during game play, if supported. Resume playing where you left off.
settings.label: Enable autosave (if supported)

settings.help: Enable real-time rewinding during game play, if supported. Press rewind or manually seek backwards using the seek bar.
settings.label: Enable rewind (if supported)

settings.help: Maximum time possible to rewind, if supported. Large rewind histories can use a lot of RAM.
settings.label: Maximum rewind time

settings.help: Enter your RetroAchievements account username
settings.label: Username

settings.help: Enter your RetroAchievements account password
settings.label: Password

settings.help: Toggle this setting to log in or out of RetroAchievements.
settings.label: Logged in

settings.help: Select the skin for the user interface. This will define the look and feel of the application.
settings.label: Skin

settings.help: Select to change the skin specific settings. Which options are available for configuration depends on the features provided by the skin.
settings.label: Configure skin...

settings.help: Change the theme associated with your selected skin.
settings.label: Theme

settings.help: Change the colours of your selected skin.
settings.label: Colours

settings.help: Choose the fonts displayed in the user interface. The font sets are configured by your skin.
settings.label: Fonts

settings.help: Resize the view of the user interface.
settings.label: Zoom

settings.help: Defines the strength of the stereoscopic 3D effect in the GUI. This is done by controlling the depth of perception within the GUI, so the higher the value, the more elements will pop out of the screen. [Zero] Disables the stereoscopic 3D effect of the GUI.[CR]For a good visual experience, the value should be higher for small screens and lower for large screens. Note: this is not supported by all skins.
settings.label: Stereoscopic 3D effect strength

settings.help: Turn this off to remove the scrolling RSS news ticker.
settings.label: Show RSS news feeds

settings.help: Edit the RSS feeds.
settings.label: Edit

settings.help: Chooses the language of the user interface.
settings.label: Language

settings.help: Choose which character set is used for displaying text in the user interface. This doesn't change the character set used for subtitles, for that go to Player > Language.
settings.label: Character set

settings.help: Select virtual keyboard layouts.
settings.label: Virtual keyboard layouts

settings.help: Select the formats for temperature, time and date. The available options depend on the selected language.
settings.label: Region default format

settings.help: Select country location.
settings.label: Timezone country

options.label: South Georgia & the South Sandwich Islands
options.value: South Georgia & the South Sandwich Islands

settings.help: Select your current timezone.
settings.label: Timezone

settings.help: Choose which short date format is used for displaying the date in the user interface.
settings.label: Short date format

settings.help: Choose which long date format is used for displaying the date in the user interface.
settings.label: Long date format

settings.help: Choose which time format is used for displaying the time in the user interface.
settings.label: Time format

settings.help: Choose whether to use 12 or 24-hour format for displaying the time in the user interface.
settings.label: Use 12 / 24-hour format

settings.help: Choose which temperature unit is used for displaying temperatures in the user interface.
settings.label: Temperature unit

settings.help: Choose which speed unit is used for displaying speeds in the user interface.
settings.label: Speed unit

settings.help: Select the screensaver. The "Dim" screensaver will be forced when fullscreen video playback is paused or a dialogue box is active.
settings.label: Screensaver mode

settings.help: Select to change the screensaver specific settings. Which options are available for configuration depends on the features provided by the screensaver add-on.
settings.label: Settings

settings.help: Preview the selected screensaver.
settings.label: Preview

settings.help: Set the time to wait for any activity to occur before displaying the screensaver.
settings.label: Wait time

settings.help: If music is being played, the screensaver will never be activated
settings.label: Disable screensaver when playing audio

settings.help: Dim the display when media is paused. Not valid for the "Dim" screensaver mode.
settings.label: Use dim if paused during video playback

settings.help: Here you can enable or disable master lock and define the PIN code used to unlock it. You can also specify which areas of the application would need a PIN to access them.
settings.label: Master lock code and settings

settings.help: If enabled, the master lock code is required to unlock this application on startup.
settings.label: Ask for master lock code on startup

settings.help: Select an action Kodi will perform on startup.
settings.label: Perform on startup

settings.help: Select the media window to display on startup.
settings.label: Startup window

settings.help: This provides access to where video sources can be added and otherwise managed.
settings.label: Videos...

settings.help: This provides access to where music sources can be added and otherwise managed.
settings.label: Music...

settings.help: This provides access to where picture sources can be added and otherwise managed.
settings.label: Pictures...

settings.help: Check for new media files on startup.
settings.label: Update library on startup

settings.help: Hide the library scanning progress bar during scans.
settings.label: Hide progress of library updates

settings.help: Select scanner action for different video versions.[CR][Enabled] Add different video versions to library seperately without confirmation.[CR][Disabled] Prompt for converting different video versions into additional versions of the original.
settings.label: Ignore different video versions on scan

settings.help: Select scanner action for video extras in "extras" folder.[CR][Enabled] Scan video extras like regular videos.[CR][Disabled] Add video extras to library for associated video.
settings.label: Ignore video extras on scan

settings.help: Remove items from your library that can't be found (either renamed, deleted, or on removable storage that is currently unplugged).
settings.label: Clean library

settings.help: Export the video library database to XML files. This will optionally overwrite your current XML files.
settings.label: Export library

settings.help: Import an XML file into the video library database.
settings.label: Import library

settings.help: Check for new and removed media files on startup.
settings.label: Update library on startup

settings.help: Hide the library scanning progress bar during scans.
settings.label: Hide progress of library updates

settings.help: Remove items from your library that can't be found (either renamed, deleted, or on removable storage that is currently unplugged).
settings.label: Clean library

settings.help: Export parts of the music library to an XML file or NFO files. This will optionally overwrite your current NFO and artwork files.
settings.label: Export library

settings.help: Import an XML file into the music library database.
settings.label: Import library

settings.help: Display the (..) item in lists for visiting the parent folder.
settings.label: Show parent folder items

settings.help: Ignore certain tokens, e.g. "The", during sort operations. "The Simpsons" would for example be sorted as "Simpsons".
settings.label: Ignore articles when sorting

settings.help: Show file extensions on media files, for example "You Enjoy Myself" would be shown as "You Enjoy Myself.mp3".
settings.label: Show file extensions

settings.help: Show the add source button in root sections of the user interface.
settings.label: Show "Add source" buttons

settings.help: Show hidden files and directories when listing files.
settings.label: Show hidden files and directories

settings.help: Allow files to be deleted and renamed through the user interface, via the context menu, e.g. press "C" on a keyboard to bring up this menu.
settings.label: Allow file renaming and deletion

settings.help: Toggle between [Choose], [Play] (default), [Resume], [Show information] and [Queue item].[CR][Choose] will open a context menu to select an item, e.g. show information.[CR][Play] will automatically play videos from the beginning or if a resume point is present ask whether to play from beginning or to resume.[CR][Resume] Will automatically resume videos from the last position that you were viewing them.[CR][Show information] will open the video's information dialog.[CR][Queue item] will append the video to the video playlist.
settings.label: Default select action

settings.help: Defines how to handle selection of videos with multiple versions.[CR][Enabled] Automatically select the default video version without prompting.[CR][Disabled] Display a dialogue to select a version of the video.
settings.label: Select default video version

settings.help: Toggle between [Ask if resumable] (default) and [Resume].[CR][Ask if resumable] will ask whether to play from beginning or to resume (if a resume point is present).[CR][Resume] will automatically resume videos from the last position that you were viewing them.[CR]If no resume point is set, playback will automatically start from the beginning.
settings.label: Default play action

settings.help: Enable to use embedded tags in mp4 or mkv files for library metadata. Will prevent scrapers from operating correctly.
settings.label: Use video tags

settings.help: Extract metadata information such as codec and aspect ratio from videos.
settings.label: Extract video information from files

settings.help: Extract chapter thumbnails for presentation in the chapters / bookmarks dialogue. This might increase CPU load.
settings.label: Extract chapter thumbnails

settings.help: Combines multi-part video files, DVD folders or movie folders, down to a single item in non-library views.
settings.label: Combine split video items

settings.help: When a file is scanned into the library it will display the metadata title instead of the file name.
settings.label: Replace file names with library titles

settings.help: Show "All items" entry in directory, e.g. "All albums" or "All seasons".
settings.label: Show "All items" entry

definition.help: Show information for unwatched media in the video library or hide them if not selected to prevent spoilers. Available options are 'Movie plot', 'Episode plot' and 'Episode thumb'.
definition.label: Show information for unwatched items

settings.help: Show information for unwatched media in the video library or hide them if not selected to prevent spoilers. Available options are 'Movie plot', 'Episode plot' and 'Episode thumb'.
settings.label: Show information for unwatched items

settings.help: When enabled, movies belonging to a "Movie set" are grouped together under one entry for the set in the movie library, this entry can then be opened to display the individual movies. When disabled, each movie will have its own entry in the movie library even if it belongs to a set.
settings.label: Show movie sets

settings.help: When enabled, a "Movie set" entry is used even if the movie library contains only a single movie from that set. When disabled, a "Movie set" entry is used only if the movie library contains more than one movie from that set.
settings.label: Include sets containing a single movie

settings.help: When enabled, a video with multiple versions will be shown as a folder in the video library. This folder can then be opened to display the individual video versions. When disabled, the configured select action will be applied.
settings.label: Show videos with multiple versions as folder

settings.help: Removes the title, genre, etc. nodes from the library view. Selecting a category takes you straight to the title view.
settings.label: Flatten hierarchy

settings.help: Select in which cases you want to hide the season nodes of TV shows. If hidden, selecting a TV show will directly jump into the episodes view.
settings.label: Flatten TV show seasons

settings.help: Show TV shows with no episodes when browsing the video library.
settings.label: Show empty TV shows

settings.help: When entering a TV show season or episode view automatically select the first unwatched season or episode.[CR][On first entry] The first unwatched item will be selected only when a view is entered for the first time.[CR][Always] The first unwatched item will be selected every time a view is entered.
settings.label: Select first unwatched TV show season / episode

settings.help: Whether or not to consider the items from "All seasons" and "Specials" in the unwatched item selection.
settings.label: Include "All seasons" and "Specials"

settings.help: Select the folder where movie set information (images) are saved locally.
settings.label: Movie set information folder

settings.help: When enabled, shows all performers in the artist list for music videos, not just the main artist
settings.label: Show all performers for music videos

settings.help: The amount of artwork automatically selected - [Maximum] all local images and remote art; [Basic] save space on limited devices or when using a simple skin; [Custom] configured by user for detailed control; [None] no art
settings.label: Artwork level

settings.help: Downloads thumbnails for actors from online databases when adding media to the library.
settings.label: Download actor thumbnails

settings.help: Extract thumbnails to display in library mode.
settings.label: Extract thumbnails from video files

settings.help: Show "All items" entry in directory, e.g. "All albums" or "All seasons".
settings.label: Show "All items" entry

settings.help: When enabled, both song and album artists are shown. When disabled, only album artists are shown and artists that appear only on individual songs from an album are excluded.
settings.label: Show song and album artists

settings.help: When enabled, opening a multi-disc album shows the discs as individual items instead of all the songs. Opening a disc then shows the songs on that disc.
settings.label: Split albums into individual discs

settings.help: When sorting music items by artist use sortname e.g. Parton, Dolly rather than name.
settings.label: Use artist sortname when sorting by artist

settings.help: When enabled, use the original year of release rather than the album release year (if available).
settings.label: Use original release date of albums for year

settings.help: Automatically fetch album and artist information from information providers when adding songs to the library.
settings.label: Fetch additional information during updates

settings.help: Select the folder where artist information (nfo files and images) should be saved in.
settings.label: Artist information folder

settings.help: Select the default album information provider.
settings.label: Default provider for album information

settings.help: Select the default artist information provider.
settings.label: Default provider for artist information

settings.help: With this enabled, any information that is downloaded for albums and artists will override anything you have set in your song tags, such as genres, year, song artists etc. Useful if you have MusicBrainz identifiers in your song tags.
settings.label: Prefer online information

settings.help: The amount of artwork automatically selected - [Maximum] all local images and remote art; [Basic] save space on limited devices or when using a simple skin; [Custom] configured by user for detailed control; [None] no art
settings.label: Artwork level

definition.help: The names of files that contain primary artwork (thumbnails), generally square and used both full and in reduced size to visually identify a folder, artist, album or song
definition.label: Thumbnail image files

settings.help: The names of files that contain primary artwork (thumbnails), generally square and used both full and in reduced size to visually identify a folder, artist, album or song
settings.label: Thumbnail image files

settings.help: Where no local album cover exists, online art will be used. Where neither is available, cover images embedded in music files will be used
settings.label: Prefer online album art

settings.help: Automatically go to the visualisation window when audio playback starts
settings.label: Switch to visualisation on playback

settings.help: Control the way that the names of songs are displayed in the user interface. In order to function properly, tag reading needs to be enabled.
settings.label: Track naming template

settings.help: Search for thumbs on remote shares and optical media. This can often slow down the listing of network folders.
settings.label: Search for thumbnails on remote shares

settings.help: When browsing music files in file view read the tags of those not in the music library as you go. This can make large directories slow to display, especially over a network.
settings.label: Enable tag reading in file view

settings.help: If EXIF information exists (date, time, camera used, etc.), it will be displayed.
settings.label: Show EXIF picture information

settings.help: Automatically generate picture thumbnails when entering picture folder.
settings.label: Automatically generate thumbnails

settings.help: Show videos in picture file lists.
settings.label: Show video files in listings

definition.help: Enable automatic playback of the next file in the list for selected playlist types.
definition.label: Play next video automatically

settings.help: Enable automatic playback of the next file in the list for selected playlist types.
settings.label: Play next video automatically

definition.help: Defines which step sizes to use when pressing the skip buttons. If more steps are selected for a skip direction, these can be applied by subsequent presses on the skip button within the defined skip delay. Forward (positive) and backward (negative) steps can be defined independently.
definition.label: Skip steps

settings.help: Defines which step sizes to use when pressing the skip buttons. If more steps are selected for a skip direction, these can be applied by subsequent presses on the skip button within the defined skip delay. Forward (positive) and backward (negative) steps can be defined independently.
settings.label: Skip steps

settings.help: Defines the time to wait for subsequent key presses before performing the skip. Only applies when using smart skipping (when using more than one skip step for a direction).
settings.label: Skip delay

settings.help: Allow the refresh rate of the display to be changed so that it best matches the video frame rate. This may yield smoother video playback.
settings.label: Adjust display refresh rate

settings.help: Allow the HDR mode of the display to be changed to best match the media. When disabled, Kodi applies tonemapping if needed (and if supported on your hardware) to adapt the media to the current HDR mode of the display.
settings.label: Adjust display HDR mode

settings.help: Stretch the video up to the set percentage in order to minimise black bars.
settings.label: Minimise black bars

settings.help: Select the zoom level that 4:3 videos are shown on widescreen displays.
settings.label: Display 4:3 videos as

settings.help: Adjust the method used to process and display video.
settings.label: Render method

settings.help: Use high quality scalers when upscaling a video by at least this percentage. A value below 5% makes little sense as video is processed with high GPU load without any visible picture quality improvements.
settings.label: Enable HQ scalers for scaling above

settings.help: Enable hardware video decode using Amlogic decoder.
settings.label: Allow hardware acceleration - amcodec

settings.help: Select in which mode stereoscopic 3D videos will be played.[CR][Ask me] Will show a dialogue to select the desired mode for each playback.[CR][Preferred mode] Will use the preferred mode specified in the "System -> Video hardware" section of the settings.[CR][Monoscopic / 2D] Will play the video in mono / 2D.[CR][Ignore] Disables any stereoscopic 3D processing and handling.
settings.label: Playback mode of stereoscopic 3D videos

settings.help: [Enabled] Switch GUI (and some TVs) back to 2D mode, between videos in a playlist or when playback ended.[CR][Disabled] GUI and TV will stay in stereoscopic 3D mode. For video playlists with mixed stereoscopic 3D and 2D content, then the GUI will also stay in stereoscopic 3D mode even when a non-stereoscopic 2D video is playing.
settings.label: Disable stereoscopic 3D mode when playback ended

settings.help: Enable teletext when watching a live TV stream.
settings.label: Activate teletext

settings.help: Scale teletext to 4:3 ratio.
settings.label: Scale teletext to 4:3

settings.help: Automatically plays the next item in the current folder, for example in "Files" view after a track has been played, the next track in the same folder will automatically play.
settings.label: Play next song automatically

settings.help: When songs are selected they are queued instead of playback starting immediately.
settings.label: Queue songs on selection

definition.help: Defines which step sizes to use when pressing the skip buttons. If more steps are selected for a skip direction, these can be applied by subsequent presses on the skip button within the defined skip delay. Forward (positive) and backward (negative) steps can be defined independently.
definition.label: Skip steps

settings.help: Defines which step sizes to use when pressing the skip buttons. If more steps are selected for a skip direction, these can be applied by subsequent presses on the skip button within the defined skip delay. Forward (positive) and backward (negative) steps can be defined independently.
settings.label: Skip steps

settings.help: Defines the time to wait for subsequent key presses before performing the skip. Only applies when using smart skipping (when using more than one skip step for a direction).
settings.label: Skip delay

settings.help: Smoothly fade from one audio track to the next. You can set the amount of overlap from 1-15 seconds.
settings.label: Crossfade between songs

settings.help: Allow crossfading to occur when both tracks are from the same album.
settings.label: Crossfade between songs on the same album

settings.help: Select the visualisation that will be displayed while listening to music.
settings.label: Visualisation

settings.help: Read the ReplayGain information encoded in your audio files by a program such as MP3Gain and normalise the sound levels accordingly.
settings.label: Volume adjustments

settings.help: Reference volume (PreAmp level) to use for files with encoded ReplayGain information. Default is 89dB as per standard. Change with caution.
settings.label: Files with ReplayGain information

settings.help: Reference volume (PreAmp level) to use for files without encoded ReplayGain information. Default is 89dB as per standard. Change with caution.
settings.label: Files without ReplayGain information

settings.help: Play file at lower volume, if necessary, to avoid audio limiting clipping protection. Otherwise clipping protection will be provided by the audioengine in those parts that need it.
settings.label: Avoid clipping protection on ReplayGained files

settings.help: Autorun DVD video when inserted in drive.
settings.label: Play DVDs automatically

settings.help: Force a region for DVD playback.
settings.label: Forced DVD player region

settings.help: Attempt to skip "unskippable" introductions before DVD menu.
settings.label: Attempt to skip introduction before DVD menu

settings.help: Region A - Americas, East Asia and Southeast Asia. Region B - Africa, Middle East, Southwest Asia, Europe, Australia, New Zealand. Region C - Central Asia, mainland China, Mongolia, South Asia, Belarus, Russia, Ukraine, Kazakhstan.
settings.label: Blu-ray region Code

settings.help: Specifies how Blu-rays should be opened / played back. Note: Some disc menus are not fully supported and may cause problems.
settings.label: Blu-ray playback mode

settings.help: Autorun CDs when inserted in drive.
settings.label: Audio CD insert action

settings.help: Read the information belonging to an audio CD, like song title and artist, from the Internet database gnudb.org.
settings.label: Load audio CD information from online service

settings.help: Select the location on your hard drive where ripped tracks will be saved to.
settings.label: Saved music folder

settings.help: Control how saved music is named from the tags. Tags: [B]%N[/B]: TrackNumber, [B]%S[/B]: DiscNumber, [B]%A[/B]: Artist, [B]%T[/B]: Title, [B]%B[/B]: Album, [B]%G[/B]: Genre, [B]%Y[/B]: Year, [B]%F[/B]: FileName, [B]%D[/B]: Duration, [B]%J[/B]: Date, [B]%R[/B]: Rating, [B]%I[/B]: FileSize.
settings.label: Track naming template

settings.help: Select which audio encoder to use when ripping.
settings.label: Encoder

settings.help: Configure audio encoder settings such as quality and compression level
settings.label: Settings

settings.help: Auto eject disc after rip is complete.
settings.label: Eject disc when the CD ripping is complete

settings.help: Select the amount of time that each image is displayed in a slideshow.
settings.label: Amount of time to display each image

settings.help: Images in a slideshow will pan and zoom while displayed.
settings.label: Use pan and zoom effects

settings.help: View slideshow images in a random order.
settings.label: Randomise

settings.help: Enable high quality downscaling of pictures (uses more memory and has moderate performance impact).
settings.label: High quality downscaling

settings.help: Defaults to the selected audio language if more than one language is available.
settings.label: Preferred audio language

settings.help: If enabled, audio streams that are flagged as default (and match the preferred language) are preferred over audio streams with higher quality (number of channels, codec, ...).
settings.label: Prefer default audio streams

settings.help: Defaults to the selected subtitle language if more than one language is available.
settings.label: Preferred subtitle language

settings.help: Prefer the audio stream for the visually impaired to other audio streams of the same language
settings.label: Prefer audio stream for the visually impaired

settings.help: Prefer the audio stream for the hearing impaired to other audio streams of the same language
settings.label: Prefer audio stream for the hearing impaired

settings.help: Prefer the subtitle stream for the hearing impaired to other subtitle streams of the same language
settings.label: Prefer subtitles for the hearing impaired

settings.help: Location of subtitles on the screen. [Bottom of video] / [Top of video] When possible the subtitles will be positioned within the video area (depends on video encoding). Please note that some forced positions in the subtitles cannot be changed.
settings.label: Position on screen

settings.help: Set the font type to be used for subtitles.
settings.label: Font

settings.help: Set the font size to be used for subtitles.
settings.label: Size

settings.help: Set the font style to be used for subtitles.
settings.label: Style

settings.help: Set the subtitle opacity.
settings.label: Opacity

settings.help: Set the border size.
settings.label: Border size

settings.help: Set the blur.
settings.label: Blur

settings.help: Type of background to apply to subtitles.
settings.label: Background type

settings.help: Allows you to add a margin in top and bottom aligned text. Changing this setting will affect also subtitle position set with the Video calibration.
settings.label: Vertical margin

settings.help: Override subtitles fonts in subtitle formats such as ASS / SSA. For native ASS / SSA files, this only takes effect when "Override ASS / SSA styles" is enabled.
settings.label: Override subtitles fonts

settings.help: Some subtitle formats such as SSA / ASS / WebVTT can include metadata like font style, colors, size, alignments, positions, etc. you can override the styles with your customizations (please note that side effects may occur in some cases). For native ASS / SSA files, this only takes effect when "Override ASS / SSA styles" is enabled.
settings.label: Override subtitles styles

settings.help: When enabled, your subtitle style preferences (font, colors, size, etc.) will override the native styling embedded in ASS / SSA subtitle files. When disabled, ASS / SSA subtitles are rendered with their original styles.
settings.label: Override ASS / SSA styles

settings.help: Sets the visual depth of subtitles for stereoscopic 3D videos. The higher the value, the closer the subtitles will appear to the viewer.
settings.label: Stereoscopic 3D depth of subtitles

settings.help: Set the font character set to be used for subtitles.
settings.label: Character set

settings.help: Convert PGS subtitle colors from BT.2020 PQ to SDR BT.709 for correct display. In Dolby Vision mode, the DV compositor reprocesses OSD colors, so fine-tuning settings below may have limited effect. Disable if subtitle colors appear wrong on non-HDR content.
settings.label: Use correct colors for PGS

settings.help: Controls brightness of PGS subtitles. 100% is accurate per BT.2408 (203 nits reference). Increase if subtitles appear too dim, decrease if they are too bright.
settings.label: PGS brightness

settings.help: Adjust color saturation of converted PGS subtitles. 100% is accurate. Increase if colors appear washed out, decrease to desaturate.
settings.label: PGS color saturation

settings.help: Use Reinhard soft rolloff instead of hard clipping when converting PGS subtitles. Preserves more detail in bright highlights but makes the overall image slightly darker.
settings.label: Use Reinhard tonemap for PGS

settings.help: Select the tonemapping algorithm for PGS subtitles. Classic: BT.709 gamut + per-channel tonemap. Linear: BT.709 gamut + luminance-based tonemap (preserves color ratios). Luma: BT.2020 native + luminance-based tonemap (no gamut conversion).
settings.label: PGS tonemap mode

settings.help: Scale bitmap-based subtitles (PGS, VobSub). 100% is original size. Lower values shrink, higher values enlarge.
settings.label: Bitmap subtitle zoom

settings.help: Enable to parse for CC in video stream. Puts slightly more load on the CPU
settings.label: Enable parsing for closed captions

definition.help: Set languages to use when searching for subtitles.[CR]Note: Not all subtitle services will use all languages.
definition.label: Languages to download subtitles for

settings.help: Set languages to use when searching for subtitles.[CR]Note: Not all subtitle services will use all languages.
settings.label: Languages to download subtitles for

settings.help: Specify where downloaded subtitles should be saved, the same location as the video or a custom location.
settings.label: Subtitle storage location

settings.help: Set a custom directory for your subtitles. This can be a file share.
settings.label: Custom subtitle folder

settings.help: Pause the current video while searching for subtitles and resume once the subtitle is available.
settings.label: Pause when searching for subtitles

settings.help: Automatically download first subtitle from the search result list
settings.label: Auto download first subtitle

settings.help: Select the service that will be used as default to search for TV show subtitles.
settings.label: Default TV show service

settings.help: Select the service that will be used as default to search for movie subtitles.
settings.label: Default movie service

settings.help: Display and manage available PVR client add-ons.
settings.label: PVR client add-ons

settings.help: This option will bring you to any specific settings for your PVR backend, if supported by the PVR add-on and backend.
settings.label: Client specific settings

settings.help: Clear the databases for PVR data like channels, groups, reminders and guide. Please note that not all data can be restored from the backend afterwards.
settings.label: Clear data

settings.help: Sort channel groups by the order supplied by the backend(s). If enabled, groups cannot be reordered in the Group manager
settings.label: Use channel group order from backend(s)

settings.help: Sort the channels by channel number from the backend, but use local numbering for channels.
settings.label: Use channel order from backend(s)

settings.help: Open the channel manager, which allows modifying the channel order, channel name, icon, etc.
settings.label: Channel manager

settings.help: Open the group manager, which allows modification of groups and their respective channels
settings.label: Group manager

settings.help: Instruct the backend to search for channels (if supported).
settings.label: Search for channels

settings.help: Folder where channel icons are stored.
settings.label: Folder with channel icons

settings.help: Scan for missing channel icons.
settings.label: Scan for missing icons

settings.help: Number of past days to show in the guide and to import from backends.
settings.label: Past days to display

settings.help: Number of future days to show in the guide and to import from backends.
settings.label: Future days to display

settings.help: Select what will happen when an item is selected in the guide: [Show context menu] Will trigger the context menu from where you can choose further actions; [Switch to channel] Will instantly tune to the related channel; [Show information] Will display a detailed information with plot and further options; [Record] Will create a recording timer for the selected item. ["Smart select"] The action is choosen dynamically, depending on whether the event is in the past, now or in the future.
settings.label: Default select action

settings.help: Hide "No information available" labels when no guide data is available for a channel.
settings.label: Hide "No information available" labels

settings.help: Time between imports of guide data from backends.
settings.label: Update interval

settings.help: Don't import the guide data while playing TV to minimise CPU usage.
settings.label: Prevent updates during playback

settings.help: Delete the cached guide data and reimport it from the backend.
settings.label: Clear data

settings.help: Switch to full screen display when starting playback of channels.
settings.label: Switch to full screen

settings.help: Preselect the playing channel in windows and dialogs containing channel lists. If enabled and there is a playing channel, the playing channel will be selected when opening a window or dialog containing a channel list. If disabled, the channel previously selected in a window or dialog will be selected when opening a window containing a channel list.
settings.label: Preselect playing channel in lists

settings.help: Display programming information when changing channels, such as the current TV show.
settings.label: Show channel information when switching channels

settings.help: Close the on screen display controls after switching channels.
settings.label: Close channel OSD after switching channels

settings.help: When flipping through channels using channel up/down buttons, channel switches must be confirmed using the OK button.
settings.label: Confirm channel switches by pressing "OK"

settings.help: When pressing channel up or down, the actual channel switch is delayed, allowing the user to flip to a channel number without waiting for each channel switch.
settings.label: Delay channel switch

settings.help: If set to a value greater than zero last watched time of channels will be stored the given amount of time after start of channel playback. Otherwise the last watched time will be stored immediately at start of channel playback.
settings.label: Delay mark last watched

settings.help: Display signal quality information in the codec information window (if supported by the add-on and backend).
settings.label: Show signal quality

settings.help: If enabled, this framerate is used for streams we were not able to detect fps.
settings.label: Fallback framerate

settings.help: Enable automatic playback of the next programme when playing past programmes (catchup) or for Video On Demand (VOD) channels. Please note that catchup and VOD only work if supported by the channel provider.
settings.label: Play next programme automatically

settings.help: RDS data can be used if present
settings.label: Enable RDS for radio channels

settings.help: RDS informs you about traffic advisory messages
settings.label: Message traffic advisory

settings.help: If traffic advisory is send from RDS, volume is increased
settings.label: Increase volume on traffic advisory

settings.help: Action to perform when pressing the record button. [Record current show] will record the current show from "now" to the end of the show. If no TV guide data is currently available a fixed length recording starting "now", with the value set for "Instant recording duration" will be scheduled. [Record for a fixed period of time] will schedule a fixed length recording starting "now", with the value set for "Instant recording duration". [Ask what to do] will open a dialogue containing different recording actions to choose from, like "Record current show", "Record next show" and some fixed duration recordings.
settings.label: Instant recording action

options.label: Record current show (if guide data available)
Record for a fixed period of time (Instant recording duration)

settings.help: Duration of instant recordings when pressing the record button. This value will be taken into account if "Instant recording action" is set to "Record for a fixed period of time"
settings.label: Instant recording duration

settings.help: Additional time to record before the scheduled start time to allow for minor broadcasting changes. Not supported by all add-ons and backends.
settings.label: Default start padding time

settings.help: Additional time to record after the scheduled end time to allow for minor broadcasting changes. Not supported by all add-ons and backends.
settings.label: Default end padding time

settings.help: Display a notification when timers are added, finished or removed by the backend.
settings.label: Display a notification on timer updates

settings.help: Choose a time in seconds after which PVR reminder popups will be automatically closed.
settings.label: Automatically close reminder popup after

settings.help: If enabled, a recording for the programme to remind will be scheduled when auto-closing the reminder popup, if supported by the PVR add-on and backend.
settings.label: Schedule recording when auto-closing the reminder popup

settings.help: If enabled, switch to the channel with the programme to remind when auto-closing the reminder popup.
settings.label: Switch to channel when auto-closing the reminder popup

settings.help: Execute the wakeup command below when exiting this application or going into hibernation mode. The timestamp of the next scheduled recording is passed as parameter.
settings.label: Enabled

settings.help: The command will not be executed when a recording will be started within this timeout.
settings.label: Backend idle time

settings.help: The command to execute (cmd [timestamp]).
settings.label: Wakeup command

settings.help: Time to subtract from the start time of the next scheduled recording.
settings.label: Wakeup before recording

settings.help: Execute the wakeup command every day at the given time.
settings.label: Daily wakeup

settings.help: When to execute the daily wakeup command.
settings.label: Daily wakeup time (HH:MM:SS)

settings.help: Asks for a pin code to access parental locked channels. Channels can be marked as locked in the channels editor on the general tab. Parental locked channels can't be played or recorded without entering a pin code, and the guide information is hidden for those channels.
settings.label: Enabled

settings.help: Enter a new pin code to unlock parental locked channels.
settings.label: Change PIN

settings.help: Ask for the pin code again when trying to access a parental locked channel and the code hasn't been asked for this duration.
settings.label: Unlock duration

settings.help: The name to display for this device when using various network services.
settings.label: Device name

settings.help: Allows applications on the network to discover enabled services via Zeroconf.
settings.label: Announce services to other systems

settings.help: Enable remote users to control this application through the built-in web server. Never expose the web server port to the Internet.
settings.label: Allow remote control via HTTP

settings.help: Define the web server port.
settings.label: Port

settings.help: Choose whether requests to the web server require a username and a password, which must be set below if enabled. It is recommended to always leave this setting enabled.
settings.label: Require authentication

settings.help: Define the web server username. Must be set when authentication is enabled.
settings.label: Username

settings.help: Define the web server password. Must be set when authentication is enabled.
settings.label: Password

settings.help: Enables SSL encryption in the webserver. Certificate special://userdata/server.pem and Key special://userdata/server.key must be created manually
settings.label: Enable SSL

settings.help: Select between web interfaces installed via the add-on manager.
settings.label: Web interface

settings.help: Allow programs on this device to control this application via the JSON-RPC over WebSocket, JSON-RPC over TCP, or EventServer protocol, without authentication.
settings.label: Allow remote control from applications on this system

settings.help: Allow programs on the network to control this application via the JSON-RPC over WebSocket, JSON-RPC over TCP, or EventServer protocol, without authentication. It allows anyone with access to the network to completely control this application and, therefore, this device. Never expose these interfaces to the Internet.
settings.label: Allow remote control from applications on other systems

settings.help: Enables UPnP. This allows you to stream media in your libraries to a UPnP client and to detect remote UPnP servers.
settings.label: Enable UPnP support

settings.help: Enables the UPnP server. This allows you to stream media in your libraries to a UPnP client.
settings.label: Share my libraries

settings.help: When a manual or automatic library update occurs, notify UPnP clients.
settings.label: Announce library updates

settings.help: Look for external subtitles for videos provided by the UPnP server. This can cause extensive CPU, filesystem and network load.
settings.label: Look for external subtitles

settings.help: Enable the UPnP control point. This allows you to stream media to any UPnP client and control its playback.
settings.label: Look for remote UPnP players

settings.help: If selected the remote player volume level will be synchronized with the volume of this application.[CR]Warning: Global system volume level may be different than the application volume level - they are controlled independently.[CR]This may lead the remote player to be set to 100% volume level if the application has 100% volume level but the overall system volume level is smaller.
settings.label: Synchronize volume level to remote UPnP players

settings.help: Enable the UPnP client. This allows you to stream media from any UPnP server with a control point and control playback from that server.
settings.label: Allow remote control via UPnP

settings.help: If enabled, the content from other AirPlay devices or applications can be received.
settings.label: Enable AirPlay support

settings.help: Allows volume control from AirPlay clients.
settings.label: Allow volume control

settings.help: Enables support for receiving "Videos" and "Pictures" via AirPlay. This needs to be disabled when using iOS 9 or later clients to restore music streaming via AirPlay. "Videos" and "Pictures" are only supported for iOS clients using iOS 8.x and older.
settings.label: Enable AirPlay "Videos" and "Pictures" support

settings.help: Enable AirPlay password protection.
settings.label: Use password protection

settings.help: Sets the AirPlay password.
settings.label: Password

settings.help: If a WINS server is running on the network, enter its workgroup name here. Otherwise, leave blank.
settings.label: Workgroup

settings.help: If a WINS server is running on the network, enter its IP address here. Otherwise, leave blank.
settings.label: WINS server

settings.help: Set the minimum SMB protocol version to negotiate when making connections. Forcing SMBv2 may be required to prevent SMBv1 use on some OS.
settings.label: Minimum protocol version

settings.help: Set the maximum SMB protocol version to negotiate when making connections. Forcing SMBv2 or SMBv1 compatibility may be required with older NAS and Windows shares.
settings.label: Maximum protocol version

settings.help: Force weak SMBv1 security for compatibility with the USB sharing features on some WiFi routers and NAS devices.
settings.label: Use legacy security

settings.help: Enable service to search for SMB services using the WS-Discovery protocol
settings.label: Enable WS-Discovery Service

settings.help: Data chunk size used on SMB connections
settings.label: SMB Chunk Size

settings.help: NFS protocol version to use when establishing NFS connections
settings.label: NFS Protocol Version

settings.help: Data chunk size used on NFS connections
settings.label: NFS Chunk Size

settings.help: Configure the media content to buffer.
settings.label: Buffer Mode

options.label: Only buffer true internet streams: HTTP, HTTPS, etc.
Buffer all internet filesystems, including: FTP, WebDAV, etc.
Buffer all network filesystems, including: SMB, NFS, etc.
Buffer all filesystems, including local files

settings.help: The size of the memory buffer in Mbytes. Setting zero (0) forces buffering to disk, which is not recommend on flash storage devices (eMMC, SD cards, SSD).
settings.label: Memory Size

options.label: Caches entire file on disk storage

settings.help: Determines cache fill rate in terms of avg. bitrate of stream x Read Factor. Increasing this multiple, cache fills faster. Large multiples may cause CPU spikes on some devices, saturate the connection and worsen performance.[CR][Adaptive] Uses dynamically calculated Read Factor based on cache level.
settings.label: Read Factor

settings.help: The data chunk size to use when a filesystem or protocol does not force the value, e.g. NFS will override this with its own value.
settings.label: Chunk Size

settings.help: Specify the default weather information source. See the add-ons manager for options.
settings.label: Service for weather information

settings.help: Define locations used for retrieving weather information.
settings.label: Settings

settings.help: Changes the resolution that the user interface is displayed in.
settings.label: Resolution

settings.help: Changes the refresh rate that the user interface is displayed in.
settings.label: Refresh rate

settings.help: In a multi-screen configuration, the screens not displaying this application are blacked out.
settings.label: Blank other displays

settings.help: Force display colour depth to set value if display mode does support it. Requires resolution change to get active.
settings.label: Force display colour depth

settings.help: Force colour subsampling to set value. Requires resolution change to get active.
settings.label: Force colour subsampling

settings.help: Delay of reset event after a change of refresh rate
settings.label: Delay after change of refresh rate

settings.help: Sets the base brightness level for OSD, text-based subtitles and GUI elements. Applies to both native HDR10 and DV VS10 playback. In VS10 mode, this does not affect the DV OSD peak nits setting — it scales the overall OSD brightness independently.
settings.label: OSD base brightness (HDR/DV)

definition.help: Whitelisted modes give the user the control to choose which display modes are allowed or not allowed to be used
definition.label: Whitelist

settings.help: Whitelisted modes give the user the control to choose which display modes are allowed or not allowed to be used
settings.label: Whitelist

settings.help: Select this option to allow using 3:2 pulldown refresh rates (playing 23.976 FPS video on a 59.94 Hz monitor or playing 24 FPS video on a 60 Hz monitor). You may want to use this option if your monitor doesn't have a 23.976 Hz or 24 Hz mode.
settings.label: Allow 3:2 pulldown refresh rates

settings.help: Select this option to allow using double refresh rates (playing 29.97 FPS video on a 59.94 Hz monitor or playing 30 FPS video on a 60 Hz monitor). You may want to use this option if your monitor doesn't have a 29.97 Hz or 30 Hz mode.
settings.label: Allow double refresh rates

settings.help: Changes the stereoscopic 3D mode of the user interface.
settings.label: Stereoscopic 3D mode / Current

settings.help: The preferred mode in which stereoscopic 3D media such as videos should be played.
settings.label: Preferred mode

settings.help: Calibrate the user interface by adjusting the overscan. Use this tool if the image being displayed is too large or small for your display.
settings.label: Video calibration

settings.help: Select the device to be used for playback of audio that has been decoded such as mp3.
settings.label: Audio output device

options.label: ALSA: AML-AUGESOUND, HDMI Multi Ch PCM

settings.help: Select the number of channels supported by the audio connection, or the number of speakers if connected by analogue connections. This setting does not apply to passthrough audio. Note: S/PDIF supports 2.0 channels only but can still output multichannel audio using a format supported by passthrough.
settings.label: Number of channels

settings.help: Select how the properties of the audio output are set: [Fixed] Output properties are set to the specified sampling rate & speaker configuration at all times; [Best Match] Output properties are set to always be as close a match to the source properties as possible; [Optimized] Output properties are set at the start of playback and will not change if the properties of the source change.
settings.label: Output configuration

settings.help: Set the number of volume control steps.
settings.label: Volume control steps

settings.help: Select how audio is downmixed, e.g. from 5.1 to 2.0.[CR][Enabled] Maintains volume level of the original audio source however the dynamic range is compressed.[CR][Disabled] Maintains the dynamic range of the original audio source when downmixed however volume will be lower. Note: Dynamic range is the difference between the quietest and loudest sounds in an audio source. Enable this setting if movie dialogues are barely audible.
settings.label: Maintain original volume on downmix

settings.help: Select to enable upmixing of 2 channel audio to the number of audio channels specified by the channel configuration.
settings.label: Stereo upmix

settings.help: Increase this value to make the dialogue louder compared to background sounds when downmixing multichannel audio
settings.label: Boost centre channel when downmixing

settings.help: Ignore centre and surround mix levels provided by AC3/E-AC3/DTS streams when downmixing. Fixed default levels (-3 dB, 0.7071) are used instead. Helpful when stream-provided values produce a poor downmix.
settings.label: Ignore stream downmix metadata

settings.help:
Reduces audio processing latency. Improves playback start, unpause and seek (the audio returns sooner).
You can disable this setting if you experience audio issues.
settings.label: Low latency mode

settings.help: Select the quality of resampling for cases where the audio output needs to be at a different sampling rate from that used by the source.[CR][Low] Is fast and will have minimal impact on system resources such as the use of the CPU.[CR][Medium] & [High] Will use progressively more system resources.
settings.label: Resample quality

settings.help: When the speed change exceeds this threshold, a pitch-correction filter will be applied. This avoids the "chipmonk voices" that normally result from speeding up a video
settings.label: Threshold for pitch correction

settings.help: Select the behaviour when no sound is required for either playback or GUI sounds.[CR][Always] Continuous inaudible signal is output, this keeps the receiving audio device alive for any new sounds, however this might also block sound from other applications.[CR][1-10 Minutes] Same as [Always] except that after the selected period of time audio enters a suspended state.[CR][Off] Audio output enters a suspended state. Note: Sounds can be missed if audio enters suspended state.
settings.label: Keep audio device alive

settings.help: To keep certain AVRs powered we send an inaudible random noise signal. You can disable this setting if you are using headphone or analog output.
settings.label: Send low volume noise

settings.help: If enabled, this setting will include lfe channel into the mixing when there is no dedicated LFE output channel available. This only makes sense for full range speakers.
settings.label: Include LFE when downmixing

settings.help: When including LFE in downmixing, this controls which speakers receive the LFE signal. Use 'Front L/R' to redirect LFE away from centre.
settings.label: LFE downmix target

settings.help: Configure how interface sounds are handled, such as menu navigation and important notifications.
settings.label: Play GUI sounds

settings.help: Relative volume for gui sounds
settings.label: Volume

settings.help: Select or disable the sounds used in the user interface.
settings.label: GUI sounds

settings.help: Select to allow passthrough audio for playback of compressed audio such as Dolby Digital (AC3), DTS, etc. The Client of the AudioEngine, for example Videoplayer, might decide to decode the audio stream under certain conditions. In Videoplayer case, passthrough won't be used for live streams and when you sync playback to display.
settings.label: Allow passthrough

settings.help: Select the device to be used for playback of encoded formats, these are any of the formats below in the capable receiver options.
settings.label: Passthrough output device

settings.help: Select this option if your receiver is capable of decoding Dolby Digital (AC3) streams.
settings.label: Dolby Digital (AC3) capable receiver

settings.help: Select this option if your receiver is capable of decoding Dolby Digital Plus (E-AC3) streams.
settings.label: Dolby Digital Plus (E-AC3) capable receiver

settings.help: Select this option if your receiver is capable of decoding DTS streams.
settings.label: DTS capable receiver

settings.help: Select this option if your receiver is capable of decoding TrueHD streams.
settings.label: TrueHD capable receiver

settings.help: Select this option if your receiver is capable of decoding DTS-HD streams.
settings.label: DTS-HD capable receiver

settings.help: Force the dialogue normalisation value to (0 dB) in AC-3 and E-AC-3 bitstreams. This disables receiver-side dialogue normalisation, preventing unwanted volume reduction during passthrough playback.
settings.label: AC-3 / E-AC-3 dialogue normalisation defeat (0 dB)

settings.help: Also apply dialogue normalisation defeat to E-AC-3 Dolby Atmos (DD+ Atmos) streams. This will disable Dolby Atmos object audio rendering — your receiver will fall back to standard Dolby Digital Plus surround. Safe to enable if your setup does not support Atmos.
settings.label: Include E-AC-3 Atmos (breaks Atmos)

settings.help: Force the dialogue normalisation value to (0 dB) in TrueHD Atmos bitstreams. This disables receiver-side dialogue normalisation for the Atmos presentation, preventing unwanted volume reduction during passthrough playback.
settings.label: TrueHD Atmos dialogue normalisation defeat (0 dB)

settings.help: Configure any attached peripheral devices.
settings.label: Peripherals

settings.help: View and configure peripheral add-ons.
settings.label: Driver settings

settings.help: Use a mouse or touch screen device to control the interface. Note: Disabling will cause you to lose control over this application when no keyboard or remote is present.
settings.label: Enable mouse and touch screen support

settings.help: Use a game controller to control the interface.
settings.label: Enable controller support

settings.help: Pair your controllers with the various input devices of different game systems.
settings.label: Configure attached controllers

settings.help: Select hardware keyboard layout.
settings.label: Hardware keyboard layouts

settings.help: If your Internet connection uses a proxy server, configure it here.
settings.label: Use proxy server

settings.help: Configure which proxy type is used.
settings.label: Proxy type

settings.help: Configure the proxy server address.
settings.label: Server

settings.help: Configure the proxy server port.
settings.label: Port

settings.help: Configure the proxy server username.
settings.label: Username

settings.help: Configure the proxy server password.
settings.label: Password

settings.help: If your Internet connection has limited bandwidth available, use this setting to keep bandwidth usage by this application within defined limits.
settings.label: Internet connection bandwidth limitation

settings.help: Turn off display when idle. Useful for TVs that turn off when there is no display signal detected.
settings.label: Put display to sleep when idle

settings.help: Set the time to wait for any activity to occur before shutting down.
settings.label: Shutdown function timer

settings.help: Define what action to perform when the shutdown function timer lapsed.
settings.label: Shutdown function

settings.help: Set the maximum time to wait for the network to come up after starting or waking up. When time has passed before network is up startup will continue.
settings.label: Maximum wait time for network

settings.help: Automatically send Wake-on-LAN to server(s) right before trying to access shared files or services.
settings.label: Try to wakeup remote servers on access

settings.help: Change how auto updating of add-ons are handled.
settings.label: Updates

settings.help: Show notification when an add-on have been updated.
settings.label: Show notifications

settings.help: Manage modules and support libraries that have been automatically installed as a dependency to other add-ons. Items listed as "Orphaned" are no longer required by any add-ons and are safe to uninstall.
settings.label: Manage dependencies

settings.help: Remove all modules and support libraries that have been automatically installed and are in an orphaned state.
settings.label: Remove all orphaned add-ons

settings.help: Show add-ons that are currently running in the background.
settings.label: Running

settings.help: Allow installation of add-ons from unknown sources.
settings.label: Unknown sources

settings.help: By default, add-ons from official repositories will be prevented from being auto-updated from private repositories. For cases such as updating from an add-ons beta repository this option can be switched to [Any repositories] (bear in mind this is a less secure option and enabling it could cause incompatibility and crashes).
settings.label: Update official add-ons from

settings.help: Turn debug logging on or off. Useful for troubleshooting.
settings.label: Enable debug logging

settings.help: Enables verbose messages from additional libraries to be included in the debug log.
settings.label: Enable component-specific logging

definition.help: Specify additional libraries whose verbose messages are to be included in the debug log.
definition.label: Specify component-specific logging...

options.label: Verbose logging for the [B]libcURL[/B] library (HTTP(S), DAV)

settings.help: Specify additional libraries whose verbose messages are to be included in the debug log.
settings.label: Specify component-specific logging...

settings.help: Select the folder where screenshots should be saved in.
settings.label: Screenshot folder

settings.help: Event logging allows to keep track of what has happened.
settings.label: Enable event logging

settings.help: Notification event describe regular processes and actions performed by the system or the user.
settings.label: Enable notification event logging

settings.help: Shows all events in the event log for the current profile with options to only show specific levels.
settings.label: Show event log

settings.help: Turn off the hardware noise reduction filter. Recommended for better video quality. Requires reboot.
settings.label: Disable noise reduction

settings.help: Apply a pipeline preset based on your TV's HDR capabilities. Auto uses the EDID (DV + HDR10+ support bits) to pick the best pipeline. Brand presets set the pipeline for typical behaviour of that brand: LG/Sony assume DV but no HDR10+ (enables HDR10+→DV P8.1 conversion); Samsung assumes HDR10+ only, no DV (uses Player-LED LLDV for DV sources); Panasonic/Philips/TCL assume all formats supported (no conversion). Sets DV type, HDR10+ conversion, and the 12-bit Deep Color pipeline. Leave as Manual if you want full control.
settings.label: TV preset

settings.help: Set Dolby Vision (DV) processing mode to On, On Demand, or Off. The Video Processor (VP) modes are for use with an external Video Processor such as MadVR Envy and only work with DV Mode On Demand.
settings.label: DV Mode

settings.help: Set the maximum luminance for OSD graphics (GUI, subtitles) in nits during DV playback. Controls how bright overlays appear when the DV compositor is active. Default 300 matches the compositor's built-in value.
settings.label: DV OSD peak brightness (nits)

settings.help: Select the DV output format for VP mode. VP bypasses the DV library's tonemapping and outputs the content with minimal processing. Designed for external video processors (e.g. MadVR Envy), but the HDR10 modes can also be used with displays that handle HDR10 tonemapping well. Requires DV Mode On Demand.
settings.label: DV Output Signal Type for Video Processor

settings.help: Select how Dolby Vision content is processed. Display-Led: sends the full DV stream to the TV, which handles tonemapping. Player-Led modes: the player tonemaps and outputs as DV low-latency (LLDV) or HDR10, depending on the variant. VS10 Only: disables DV tunnel and only uses VS10 format conversion.
settings.label: DV Type

options.label: Display Led (DV as DV-Std RGB 8 Bit Tunnel)

settings.help: When Off, colour space and max luminance are auto-detected from the display's EDID. When On, the max luminance below is used instead of the EDID value (colour space is still auto-detected unless manually set). Use Override EDID for full manual control.
settings.label: DV VSVDB

settings.help: Colour gamut reported to the DV engine. Should match your display's actual capability. DISPLAY uses the raw EDID primaries. Auto-detected from EDID when DV VSVDB is Off.
settings.label: Colour Space

settings.help: Peak brightness (nits) of your display, reported to the DV engine. Auto-detected from EDID when DV VSVDB is Off. Set manually when DV VSVDB is On or when the display has no EDID DV data.
settings.label: Max Lum in NITS

settings.help: When both DV and HDR10+ video streams are present, choose which video stream to output.
settings.label: Choose DV or HDR10+ video stream to output when both are present

settings.help: Converts HDR10+ dynamic metadata to DV profile 8.1 in real time, allowing the DV engine to apply scene-by-scene tonemapping for HDR10+ content.
settings.label: Convert HDR10+ video to DV profile 8.1 during playback

settings.help: Select output format when playing 8-bit SDR content. Off = passthrough. SDR BT.2020 content always bypasses VS10 to preserve the original color gamut.
settings.label: DV VS10 for SDR8 video

settings.help: Select output format when playing 10-bit SDR content. Off = passthrough. SDR BT.2020 content always bypasses VS10 to preserve the original color gamut.
settings.label: DV VS10 for SDR10 video

settings.help: Select output format when playing HDR10 content. Off = native HDR10 passthrough.
settings.label: DV VS10 for HDR10 video

settings.help: Select output format when playing HLG content. Off = native HLG passthrough.
settings.label: DV VS10 for HLG video

settings.help: Select output format when playing Dolby Vision content. DV = native DV tunnel. SDR = convert to SDR.
settings.label: DV VS10 for DV video

settings.help: Enable Level 5 active area handling for Display-Led DV playback. Level 5 metadata defines the active content area within the frame (e.g., letterbox boundaries for scope content). When enabled, shows options for sending source L5 metadata, setting L5 to zero for OSD/subtitles, and restricting subtitles to the active area.
settings.label: DV Level 5 active area handling

settings.help: Convert DV profile 7 FEL to MEL or to profile 8.1. Only use this setting if needed for audio sync issues with SoC S905X4. There is no need to use this setting with SoC S922X.
settings.label: Convert DV Profile 7 FEL to MEL or to Profile 8.1

settings.help: Append CMv4.0 extension blocks to Dolby Vision RPU metadata during playback. CMv4.0 enables display-managed tonemapping which generally produces better picture quality than CMv2.9.
settings.label: Append CMv4.0 metadata

settings.help: Show all DV/VS10 output mode options regardless of the connected display's capabilities. Use with caution.
settings.label: Force all display modes

settings.help: Skip automatic colour space and luminance detection from the display's EDID. Allows full manual control over VSVDB colour space and max luminance settings.
settings.label: Override EDID detection

settings.help: When enabled, Dolby Vision mode switching is suppressed for windowed video playback. This avoids HDMI handshakes during e.g. trailer previews in skins, but also prevents DV activation in video screensavers. Disable this if you want screensavers to trigger Dolby Vision.
settings.label: Skip DV for windowed playback

settings.help: When enabled and the TV advertises 12-bit support on 4:4:4, 4:2:2, or RGB chroma in its EDID, Kodi forces 4:2:2 12-bit HDMI output and preserves full 12-bit precision through the DV VPP during VS10 playback. Because the HDMI output is already 12-bit, no 12→10 truncation happens at the HDMI TX — no dither noise reaches the wire, so there is no oversharpening side effect on TVs with aggressive detail/edge enhancement.[CR][CR]Improves 8-bit and 10-bit content too, not just 12-bit sources: VPP scaling, tone mapping, and colour/gain math generate sub-source-bit precision that Auto would otherwise crush to 10-bit. At 4K50/60 on TVs preferring 4:4:4, Auto additionally drops HDMI output to 8-bit due to a kernel bandwidth rule — this setting works around that.[CR][CR]Interaction with the colour-space and bit-depth settings: bit depth is forced to 12-bit while this is on (overrides the bit-depth setting — disable this toggle to use 8/10/16-bit). Colour space is set to 4:2:2 only when on Auto; an explicit RGB / 4:2:0 / 4:4:4 choice wins. 4:2:2 is preferred over 4:4:4 because it sidesteps the 4K50/60 bandwidth clamp; chroma-detail loss vs 4:4:4 is invisible on video content.[CR][CR]Falls back silently to Auto if the sink does not support 12-bit on a non-4:2:0 chroma. Enabled by default.
settings.label: 12-bit Deep Color pipeline

settings.help: Select the type of audio correction to fix seamless branching audio drop and audio sync issues. Different equipment will work better with different options, so try each option to see what works best for your setup.
settings.label: Audio Fixes mode

settings.help: Reset the PTS timestamp correction offset when seeking. Fixes subtitle desync after multiple seeks, but introduces a slightly "jumpy" A/V sync behaviour after seeking, which is not a big issue and usually desirable over subtitle desync.
settings.label: Reset PTS offset on seek

settings.help: Drive the playback reference clock from real HDMI vsync events instead of the system clock. Improves timing accuracy and reduces micro-hitches. Disable to fall back to the legacy system-clock based timing.
settings.label: Use display vsync as playback reference clock

settings.help: Seek to the nearest video keyframe instead of the exact requested time. With hardware decoding, frame-accurate seeking has to decode a full GOP of pre-target frames before reaching the requested time, which is visible as a brief fast-forward or stutter after seek. Keyframe seeking lands up to one GOP-length earlier than requested but plays smoothly. Disable for frame-accurate seeking at the cost of post-seek catch-up artefacts.
settings.label: Fast (keyframe-accurate) seeking
